title: 10 best herbal tinctures for stye
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for stye are liquid extracts of medicinal herbs, prepared by soaking the plant material in alcohol or glycerin. These tinctures can help alleviate the discomfort and inflammation associated with styes. Examples of herbal tinctures that are beneficial for styes include calendula, goldenseal, and Echinacea. Calendula has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can reduce swelling and fight infection. Goldenseal contains berberine, which has antimicrobial properties that can help treat the infection. Echinacea boosts the immune system, helping the body fight off the infection more quickly. By using these herbal tinctures, individuals can experience faster healing, reduced pain and swelling, and a quicker return to their daily activities.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eyebright tincture helps with stye because it contains anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can reduce swelling, redness, and pain associated with styes. The herb, Euphrasia officinalis, is rich in flavonoids, tannins, and phenolic acids that have a soothing effect on the eyelid and can help speed up the healing process. Additionally, eyebright tincture can help alleviate irritation and itching, providing relief to individuals suffering from this common eye condition. Its astringent properties can also help dry up the pus accumulated in the stye, promoting quicker recovery.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Eyebright tincture reduces inflammation, which is important for stye as it helps decrease swelling, redness, and pain. This property is attributed to the presence of active constituents such as flavonoids, tannins, and phenolic acids.

Antimicrobial: Eyebright tincture inhibits the growth of bacteria, which is crucial for stye since it is commonly caused by Staphylococcus aureus. The active constituents responsible for this property include aucubin, eugenol, and essential oils.

Astringent: Eyebright tincture tightens and tones the skin, which aids in the healing process of stye by reducing discharge and promoting skin integrity. This property is primarily due to the high tannin content in eyebright.

Analgesic: Eyebright tincture alleviates pain, which is essential for stye as it often causes discomfort and irritation. The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in eyebright contribute to its analgesic properties.

Immune-boosting: Eyebright tincture supports the immune system, which is important for stye as it helps the body fight off infection and promotes overall healing. This property can be attributed to the presence of vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants in eyebright.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of dried eyebright herb and place it in a clean glass jar.
Pour 10 ounces of vodka or rum over the herb, ensuring it is fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it daily.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into a dark glass bottle, discarding the solid material.
Label the bottle with the name, ingredients, and date, and store it in a cool, dark place for up to a year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tincture helps with stye because it has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can reduce inflammation and fight off the bacteria that cause styes. The herb, also known as Calendula officinalis, contains flavonoids, which are powerful antioxidants that help to speed up the healing process. Applying pot marigold tincture to the affected area can help to alleviate the pain, reduce redness and swelling, and promote the drainage of any pus or discharge. Its soothing and calming effects can also help to prevent the spread of the infection to other parts of the eye. Regular use of pot marigold tincture can help to accelerate the healing process and provide relief from the symptoms of styes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tincture helps reduce inflammation in the eyelid, providing relief from stye symptoms, due to the presence of flavonoids and carotenoids.
Antimicrobial: Its antimicrobial properties, attributed to the compound thiophene, help combat the bacterial infection causing stye.
Wound-healing: The tincture promotes wound healing, reducing the size and redness of the stye, thanks to its high antioxidant content and essential oils.
Analgesic: Pot marigold tincture has pain-relieving properties, alleviating discomfort and irritation caused by stye, resulting from its flavonoid and triterpene constituents.
Immune-boosting: The tincture strengthens the immune system, aiding in faster recovery from stye, due to the presence of various vitamins, including A, C, and E.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 handfuls of fresh pot marigold flowers and leaves, rinse, and let dry.
Chop herbs and place in a clean jar, filling it 3/4 full, then add 80-proof vodka until herbs are completely submerged.
Seal jar tightly and store in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain tincture through cheesecloth into a clean jar, squeezing out excess liquid.
Label jar with the name, date, and dosage (e.g. 1-2 droppersful up to 3 times daily) and store in a cool, dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St. John's wort tincture helps with stye because of its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The herb contains several active compounds, including hypericin and hyperforin, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and fight off bacterial infections. When applied topically to the affected area, the tincture can help reduce swelling, redness, and pain associated with a stye. Additionally, St. John's wort has been shown to have wound-healing properties, which can help speed up the recovery process. Overall, the herb's unique combination of therapeutic benefits makes it a valuable natural remedy for treating styes and promoting eye health.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial: St. John's wort tincture has antimicrobial properties that help reduce the growth of bacteria responsible for stye infections, thanks to its active constituents like hypericin and hyperforin.
Anti-inflammatory: Its anti-inflammatory properties help decrease swelling, redness, and pain associated with styes, due to the presence of hypericin, hyperforin, and flavonoids.
Analgesic: St. John's wort tincture can provide pain relief for styes, as it contains compounds like hypericin, hyperforin, and essential oils that have analgesic effects.
Wound healing: The tincture promotes wound healing and tissue regeneration, reducing the duration of stye symptoms, due to its active constituents such as tannins, flavonoids, and essential oils.

Immunomodulatory: St. John's wort tincture can help regulate the immune response, supporting the body's ability to fight off stye-causing infections, through the action of its active ingredients, including hypericin, hyperforin, and flavonoids.
remedy_recipe: Harvest St. John's Wort flowers and leaves, ensuring they are fully open and dry, typically in mid-summer.
Chop or crush the herb to increase surface area, then fill a clean jar 3/4 full with the chopped herb.
Pour 100-proof vodka or rum over the herb until it is completely submerged, then secure the lid and store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it occasionally.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into a clean, dark glass bottle or jar, squeezing out the excess liquid from the herb.
Label the bottle with the name of the tincture, the date, and the recommended dosage (e.g., 20-30 drops in water, up to 3 times a day) and store it in a cool, dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain tincture helps with stye because it has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can reduce swelling, redness, and pain associated with styes. The herb's active compounds, such as allantoin, aid in healing and soothing irritated skin. Plantain tincture can also help to decrease the inflammation caused by the blocked oil gland, allowing the stye to drain and heal faster. Its soothing effects can provide relief from the discomfort and itchiness that often accompany a stye. Regular application of plantain tincture can help prevent the recurrence of styes and promote overall eye health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Plantain tincture helps reduce inflammation in the eyelid, providing relief from stye symptoms, due to the presence of flavonoids and iridoid glycosides.
Antimicrobial: Its antimicrobial properties, attributed to allantoin and tannins, help combat the bacterial infection causing stye.
Wound healing: Plantain tincture promotes wound healing, thanks to allantoin and mucilage, which accelerate tissue regeneration and protect the damaged area.
Analgesic: The analgesic effect of plantain tincture, owing to its flavonoid content, helps alleviate pain and discomfort associated with stye.
Astringent: Plantain tincture's astringent properties, due to tannins, help reduce redness and soothe irritated skin around the stye, providing comfort and promoting recovery.
remedy_recipe: Harvest plantain leaves (Plantago major) from a clean, pesticide-free source, rinse, and dry thoroughly.
Chop or grind 2 ounces of dried plantain leaves and place them in a clean, dry glass jar.
Pour 16 ounces of 80-100 proof vodka or rum over the plantain leaves, ensuring they are fully submerged.
Securely cover the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.

Strain the tincture using cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, and store the final product in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid. Use a few drops in the affected eye, up to 3 times a day, for stye treatment.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chickweed tincture helps with stye because it has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can reduce swelling, redness, and pain associated with styes. Chickweed contains various beneficial compounds, such as saponins, flavonoids, and tannins, that can help to soothe irritated skin and promote healing. The tincture can be applied directly to the affected area to provide rapid relief and accelerate the recovery process. Chickweed's natural healing properties make it an effective and safe alternative to over-the-counter medications for treating styes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Chickweed tincture helps reduce inflammation in the eye area, providing relief from stye-related swelling and redness, thanks to its active constituents, including triterpene saponins and flavonoids.
Antimicrobial: Its antimicrobial properties help fight off the bacterial infection causing the stye, as chickweed tincture contains active compounds like tannins, essential oils, and saponins.

Analgesic: Chickweed tincture can alleviate pain and discomfort associated with styes due to its natural analgesic properties, attributable to its active constituents like salicylic acid and rosmarinic acid.

Demulcent: The demulcent property of chickweed tincture soothes irritated skin and mucous membranes, reducing itching and promoting healing of the stye, due to the presence of mucilage and polysaccharides.

Circulation-stimulating: Chickweed tincture improves blood circulation in the affected area, facilitating the removal of toxins and promoting faster healing, owing to its active compounds like glycosides and saponins.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh chickweed, filling a clean jar 3/4 full, ensuring it's identifiably Stellaria media.
Submerge chickweed with enough 100-proof vodka to cover herbs by 1-2 inches, then secure a lid on the jar.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, gently shaking it once a day.
Strain the tincture using cheesecloth into a dark glass bottle, squeezing out all liquid from chickweed.
Label the bottle with the preparation date, dosage (typically 20-30 drops up to 3 times a day), and store in a cool, dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ground ivy tincture helps with stye because it has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can reduce swelling and fight off infection. Styes are typically caused by a bacterial infection of the oil glands in the eyelid, which can result in a red, painful bump. The herbal tincture made from ground ivy, also known as Glechoma hederacea, contains compounds that can help to soothe the irritation and promote healing. By reducing inflammation and fighting bacteria, ground ivy tincture can help to alleviate the symptoms of a stye and promote a faster recovery.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Ground ivy tincture helps reduce inflammation in the eye, providing relief from stye symptoms, due to its active constituents such as flavonoids and phenolic acids.
Antimicrobial: Its antimicrobial properties, attributed to rosmarinic acid and other phenolic compounds, help combat stye-causing bacterial infections.
Analgesic: Ground ivy tincture alleviates pain associated with styes, as it contains compounds like luteolin and apigenin that have pain-relieving properties.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant effects of ground ivy, resulting from its rich polyphenol content, aid in protecting the eye from oxidative stress and promoting healing.

Circulatory stimulant: Ground ivy tincture improves blood circulation in the affected area, helping to clear blockages and reduce stye formation, thanks to its active constituents like essential oils and saponins.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ground ivy leaves, rinse, and dry thoroughly, ensuring they are completely free of dirt and other contaminants.
Chop or grind the leaves into small pieces, then fill a clean jar halfway with the herb, leaving enough room for the menstruum.

Prepare a menstruum using a 1:2 ratio of alcohol (80-100 proof) to water; for example, use 2 cups of alcohol for every 1 cup of water, then pour the menstruum over the ground ivy leaves in the jar, ensuring they are fully submerged.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for two to four weeks, shaking it daily to facilitate the extraction process.

After the recommended maceration period, strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, squeezing out any excess liquid; store the resulting tincture in a dark glass bottle, keeping it in a cool, dark place for up to two years. Apply the tincture topically to the affected area for stye relief, using a recommended dosage of 2-3 drops, 3 times a day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tincture helps with stye because it has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can reduce swelling, redness, and pain associated with styes. The herb's active compounds, such as flavonoids and alkaloids, can help fight off the bacterial infection that causes styes, while also promoting healing and reducing the risk of scarring. Additionally, yarrow tincture can improve blood circulation, which can help speed up the healing process and alleviate discomfort. Its astringent properties can also help dry out the stye, reducing its size and promoting drainage.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and redness in the affected area, providing relief from stye discomfort. This is due to the presence of compounds like flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones.

Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of yarrow tincture help fight bacterial infections that can cause or worsen styes, thanks to the active constituents like azulene, salicylic acid, and tannins.
Astringent: Yarrow tincture's astringent properties can help dry up excess fluid and reduce swelling in the stye, promoting healing. This is attributed to the tannins found in yarrow.

Analgesic: Yarrow tincture has natural pain-relieving properties that can alleviate the discomfort and pain associated with styes. This is due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects.

Immune-boosting: Yarrow tincture can help strengthen the immune system, making it easier for the body to fight off infections that can lead to styes. This is attributed to the plant's high antioxidant content, including flavonoids and phenolic acids.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 handfuls of fresh yarrow flowers and leaves, ensuring they are fully grown and free from pesticides.
Chop the yarrow material finely and place it in a clean, dry glass jar, filling it up to three-quarters.
Pour 80-proof vodka or rum over the chopped yarrow until it is completely submerged, sealing the jar tightly.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it gently once a day.
Strain the liquid using a cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, squeezing out the excess, and store the final tincture in a dark glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rue tincture helps with stye because it has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling, redness, and pain in the affected area. Rue, also known as Ruta graveolens, contains alkaloids and flavonoids that have been shown to inhibit the growth of Staphylococcus aureus, the bacterium responsible for causing styes. Additionally, rue tincture can help stimulate circulation and promote the healing of damaged tissue, making it an effective natural remedy for treating styes. Its potent properties make it a popular choice for those seeking a gentle yet effective alternative to over-the-counter treatments.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Rue tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and redness in the affected area, providing relief from stye symptoms (active constituents
Antimicrobial: Rue tincture's antimicrobial properties help fight bacterial infections causing styes, promoting healing and preventing further infection (active constituents
Analgesic: Rue tincture's analgesic properties help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with styes, making it easier for individuals to go about their daily activities (active constituents
Astringent: Rue tincture's astringent properties help shrink and tighten the skin around the stye, reducing the size and promoting faster healing (active constituents
Circulation-boosting: Rue tincture's circulation-boosting properties help increase blood flow to the affected area, promoting healing and reducing the risk of complications (active constituents
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rue herb, filling a clean jar with roughly 2 cups of chopped leaves and flowers, then bruise the herb gently with a wooden spoon.
Pour 100 proof vodka or rum over the herb until it is completely submerged, covering the jar with a tight-fitting lid.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for two to four weeks, shaking it daily to ensure proper extraction.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve, squeezing out the excess liquid and discarding the spent herb.
Store the finished rue tincture in a clean, dark glass bottle, keeping it in a cool, dry place for up to a year. Use a dropper to apply the tincture directly to the stye, repeating as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme tincture helps with stye because it has powerful antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. Thyme contains a compound called thymol, which is known to inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause styes. Additionally, thyme's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and redness associated with styes. Applying thyme tincture to the affected area can help alleviate symptoms and promote healing. Its natural properties make it a popular alternative to over-the-counter treatments, and it can be used safely in conjunction with other remedies.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Thyme tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and redness in the affected area of a stye, thanks to its active constituents like flavonoids and volatile oils.
Antimicrobial: Thyme tincture's antimicrobial properties help fight off the bacterial infection causing the stye, due to the presence of thymol and carvacrol.
Analgesic: Thyme tincture's analgesic properties help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with a stye, as it contains compounds like thymol and flavonoids.
Antioxidant: Thyme tincture's antioxidant properties support healing and reduce oxidative stress in the affected area, due to the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins.
Immune-boosting: Thyme tincture's immune-boosting properties help the body fight off infections and support overall eye health, as it contains various essential vitamins and minerals.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thyme leaves, filling a clean, dry mason jar halfway, lightly packing them down.
Pour 100-proof vodka over the thyme leaves until completely submerged, covering the jar with a tight-fitting lid.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for two to four weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
After the designated time, strain the mixture using cheesecloth into a clean, dark glass bottle, squeezing out any excess liquid.
Label the bottle with the contents, date, and suggested dosage (apply 1-2 drops directly to the stye, 2-3 times daily).

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oregano tincture helps with stye because it has powerful antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling, redness, and pain associated with styes. The active compounds in oregano, such as carvacrol and thymol, have been shown to inhibit the growth of Staphylococcus aureus, the bacterium responsible for causing styes. By applying oregano tincture topically, it can help to eliminate the infection and promote healing, while also soothing the affected area and providing relief from discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Oregano tincture can help fight bacteria causing a stye, thanks to its high concentration of carvacrol and thymol.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Oregano tincture helps reduce inflammation and swelling in the affected area, due to the presence of compounds like rosmarinic acid and ursolic acid.
Pain-relieving properties: The analgesic effects of oregano tincture, attributed to its terpene content, can alleviate pain and discomfort caused by a stye.
Antioxidant properties: Oregano tincture's high antioxidant content, primarily from rosmarinic acid, aids in healing and tissue repair around the stye.

Immune-boosting properties: By strengthening the immune system, oregano tincture can help the body fight off infections and speed up stye recovery, as it contains immune-enhancing compounds like carvacrol and thymol.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 cups of fresh oregano leaves and stems, placing them in a clean jar.
Pour 10 ounces of vodka or rum over the oregano, ensuring all plant material is submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
After the designated time, strain the mixture using cheesecloth into a dark glass bottle, squeezing out the liquid from the oregano.
Label the bottle with the ingredients, date, and recommended dosage (e.g., 20-30 drops up to 3 times a day) and store in a cool, dark place.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat stye with herbal tinctures is by using a goldenseal tincture, which has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can help reduce swelling and fight infection. Apply the tincture directly to the stye with a clean cotton swab several times a day. Additionally, calendula tincture can be used for its anti-inflammatory and wound-healing benefits, promoting faster recovery. Always ensure the tinctures are pure and of high quality, and consult a healthcare professional if symptoms persist.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to stye that are treated with herbal tinctures include chalazion and blepharitis. Chalazion is a cyst that forms on the eyelid, caused by a blocked oil gland. Herbal tinctures like Euphrasia and Calendula can reduce inflammation and promote healing. Blepharitis is an inflammation of the eyelids, often caused by bacteria or scalp dandruff. Goldenseal and eyebright tinctures are commonly used to treat blepharitis due to their antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. However, it's important to consult a healthcare professional before starting any herbal treatment to ensure safety and effectiveness.
